channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
item.description: AI Engineer: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an approach to convert notes, documents, videos, and repositories into memory representations for AI agents. The technique enables agents to leverage stored information for reasoning and decision-making.

item.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
item.description: AICodeKing: Ornith is a new family of open agentic coding models from DeepReinforce, ranging from 9B to 397B parameters, built on Gemma and Qwen. The models are designed for coding, tool use, and agentic workflows, with MIT licensing and GGUF versions available for local deployment.

item.title: How to Create a Client Pitch With AI — From Prompt to PDF in 15 Minutes
item.description: AI Master: A video demonstration showing how to generate a client pitch one-pager from a single prompt to finished PDF in 15 minutes using Venngage AI. The workflow covers content generation, design, editing, visuals, and export functionality for professional marketing documents.

item.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
item.description: AI Engineer: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa discusses production deployment of AI agents serving state and local governments. The talk covers agent architecture, A2A protocol, Effect-TS tooling, evaluation frameworks, long context handling, monitoring, and integration with Claude and Cursor.

item.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
item.description: AI Engineer: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents are severely limited because they lack persistent context and memory across interactions, comparing them to an expert engineer with amnesia who can only see small portions of code. He advocates for fixing this fundamental architectural problem in agentic systems.

item.title: Fable 5 Returns... This Is Big
item.description: AI Revolution: A YouTube video discussing leaked evidence that Anthropic's Fable 5 model may be returning after being pulled for national security reasons. The video points to Claude Code leaks, Amazon Bedrock confirmations, Congressional demands for transparency, and reports of a stronger successor model as signals that the situation may be shifting.

item.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
item.description: AI Engineer: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity and state should be anchored in session logs rather than in orchestration layers, tools, or LLMs. As models commoditize, durable append-only logs become the key differentiator, enabling durability, continuity, forkability, and portability across different runtimes and model providers.

item.title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
item.description: AI Engineer: Raymond Weitekamp discusses how recursive language model patterns at inference time can be mapped onto coding agents to improve performance and reliability. The approach uses recursive computation patterns to enhance agent behavior on code-generation tasks.

item.title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian and author Ada Palmer on the concept of forgiveness in history. The title suggests an examination of how forgiveness functioned as a social or political mechanism.

item.title: GTA VI + GPT-6: Rockstar's AI Mechanics Revealed
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video analyzing GTA VI's actual AI architecture based on patent filings and job postings, debunking viral "GTA VI + GPT-6" claims. The creator claims Rockstar has built superior real-time behavioral simulation systems independent of major AI labs and compares LLM outputs across platforms.

item.title: What's new with data agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech presented enhancements to data analytics agents with new capabilities for data engineering, data science, and conversational analytics. The session covered new developer tools including the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations, accessible via Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and IDEs like VSCode.

item.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Nishant Gupta from Meta discusses production evaluation approaches for autonomous AI agents that go beyond offline benchmarks. The talk covers continuous evaluation pipelines, online vs offline evaluation architectures, human-in-the-loop systems, and methods for detecting drift and hallucinations in agentic workflows at scale.

item.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
item.description: AI Engineer: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents raise engineering practice to the system level rather than eliminating it. She contends that core engineering disciplines like systems thinking, decomposition, and state management remain essential when designing agentic systems.

item.title: Anthropic's Labs Lead On Fable's Capabilities + Building AI-Native Products — With Mike Krieger
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Mike Krieger, head of Anthropic Labs and Instagram co-founder, discusses Anthropic's frontier models Fable and Mythos during a period when the government forced the company to pull them from the market. Krieger describes how Fable enables new workflows like queuing up full nights of work before bed, and characterizes Anthropic's safety warnings as material rather than marketing.

item.title: Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: AI-native databases enable intelligent agents by providing enterprise data access through AI-powered search, natural language interfaces, and multimodal queries. These systems support graph retrieval-augmented generation to deliver high-fidelity interconnected context, with interoperability via Model Context Protocol and agentic IDEs.

item.title: Boost AI context with hybrid search in Spanner
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Spanner now supports hybrid search combining full-text and vector search for AI-powered applications. The feature integrates with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform to enable contextual search and retrieval-augmented generation workflows.

item.title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud session on Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) for standardized agent-to-data connections. Covers building agents that access BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB with integrated observability for performance and cost tracking.

item.title: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented the Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Agent Registry for building connected AI agents that can access organizational data across SaaS and on-premises systems. The session covers using Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers for tool integration and building custom MCP servers to transform APIs into agent-ready tools.

item.title: What's new with Gemini from Google DeepMind
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind presented advances in Gemini 3, featuring upgraded model reasoning, expanded context windows, and enhanced multimodal tool use. The presentation focused on agentic applications and agent design research advancements in Vertex AI.

item.title: Building an AI app: A low-code guide for small teams
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud offers low-code development tools for building AI applications with natural language interfaces and hybrid search capabilities. The platform leverages Cloud SQL, AlloyDB for PostgreSQL, and Gemini to enable small teams without AI expertise to deploy intelligent apps quickly.

item.title: What's new in Looker: Empowering business users in the governed agentic era
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced new features in Looker including dashboard agents that automate workflows and insights, conversational analytics, and semantic model evolutions. The platform grounds AI in a governed semantic layer to enable business intelligence in the agentic era.

item.title: NoSQL for modern apps and AI: The future of Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented advancements in its NoSQL database services Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable for modern and AI workloads. The session covered product releases, architectural innovations, and roadmaps designed to support agentic and AI applications with high performance and scalability.

item.title: Implementing DeepMind innovation: Deep research API
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind's Deep Research API enables agents powered by Gemini to perform comprehensive research tasks and improve performance. The API can be implemented in applications using Google Cloud.

item.title: What's new in Cloud Run
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates new Cloud Run capabilities for deploying AI agents and inference workloads on a serverless platform. The session covers features for scaling applications seamlessly while reducing operational overhead to support AI-driven development.

item.title: What's new in Cloud SQL: Drive performance, high availability, and security
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud SQL adds AI-driven automated health management and fleet-wide insights for MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server databases. The update emphasizes performance optimization, high availability, security enhancements, and low-risk cloud migration via Database Migration Service.

item.title: What's new in Google Cloud's agent platform
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced an updated agent platform with unified Agent Builder featuring no-code/low-code design, Agent Registry for reusable components, enterprise governance with time-travel debugging, and integrated evaluation and simulation capabilities. The platform supports multi-agent workflows and agent-to-agent communication.

item.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
item.description: AI Engineer: Jacob E. Thomas identifies Miranda distortion, a structural failure where persona systems conflate cultural composites with primary sources because training data contains vastly more cultural representation than documentary record. He proposes Prism Experiment, a four-stage paradigm shift from cognitive to epistemic simulation, using temporally-anchored evaluation with domain expert assessment across anachronism detection, documentary consistency, and contextual plausibility.

item.title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) has introduced innovations for AI training, serving, reinforcement learning, and agentic workflows. The platform now addresses evolving AI infrastructure requirements including cost efficiency, scalability, and reliability for complex workloads beyond frontier model serving.

item.title: Engineering the future of Kubernetes for AI at scale
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud discusses Kubernetes evolution for AI-scale workloads, emphasizing hardware-aware platforms, infrastructure elasticity, agentic workflow security, and resource optimization. Google frames itself as the leading Kubernetes contributor steering industry direction for 11 consecutive years.

item.title: Unsure how to approach CS50? Carter Zenke answers.
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming offered through multiple channels including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare. The course is taught by David J. Malan and maintains active communities across numerous social platforms.

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code: THIS is VERY CRAZY MAN!
item.description: AICodeKing: Moonshot AI launched Kimi K2.7 Code, a coding-focused agentic model with a Mixture-of-Experts architecture and one trillion parameters. The model supports a 256K context window and includes a CLI tool for repository exploration, file editing, and command execution.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held its final day of main-stage programming on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The livestream featured keynotes, talks, and a startup battlefield competition, serving as a major industry gathering point.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 is a conference held at Moscone West in San Francisco on July 1, 2026. The event features keynote sessions and main-stage programming throughout the day, livestreamed online.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1: Software Factories & Keynotes
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held on June 30, 2026 in San Francisco featured keynote sessions and main-stage programming on software factories and related AI engineering topics. The event was livestreamed online and included multiple programming blocks throughout the day at Moscone West.

item.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities - BBC
item.description: Google News AI: Anthropic has accused Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities, according to a BBC report. The accusation suggests unauthorized access to or replication of AI model functionality.

item.title: Ask HN: Where is our profession (programmer) going?
item.description: syntaxbush: A programmer observes that at a visited software company, LLMs like Claude have become the primary tool for code generation and problem-solving, with human code review, abstraction practices, and deep problem understanding being de-emphasized. The author expresses concern that this represents a shift from precise, knowledge-intensive work toward probabilistic, outsourced decision-making.

item.title: The Agentic OS Setup That Will 10x Claude Code
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI argues that building an Agentic OS setup enhances Claude Code performance and provides benefits including custom control, visual interfaces, and non-technical team accessibility. The video covers four levels of Agentic OS architecture and its relationship to loop engineering and memory management.

item.title: [AINews] It's Meta-Harness Summer
item.description: Latent Space: Latent Space discusses a shift toward Meta's harness approaches for evaluating AI systems, suggesting Meta-based testing harnesses are becoming the dominant standard in the industry. The piece presents this as a significant trend shift in AI evaluation methodology.

item.title: Bible as RAG Database
item.description: jacksonastone: A discussion or exploration of using the Bible as a retrieval-augmented generation database. This applies RAG techniques to religious text retrieval and reference systems.

item.title: What I'm Finding About LLM Code Style and Token Costs
item.description: jimmont: The author reports observations about how different code styles affect token consumption when using LLMs. This is a practical analysis of code formatting patterns and their impact on inference cost, based on experimental findings.

item.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Europe is resisting U.S. export restrictions on semiconductor manufacturing equipment as part of Washington's broader strategy to contain Chinese chip capabilities. ASML CEO notes that current Chinese access is limited to older deep ultraviolet lithography tools from a decade ago, which the MATCH Act would further restrict.

item.title: We'll fight the platform war against Big AI
item.description: aendruk: An author signals intent to challenge the dominance of large AI platforms and incumbent companies. The framing suggests a platform competition dynamic between centralized AI incumbents and alternative approaches.

item.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin analyzes Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model accounts for only 10% of results while the surrounding harness, context engineering, tools, and verification systems determine the other 90%. The video covers the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles, and token economics implications for 2026 software development.

item.title: simonw/browser-compat-db
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison created simonw/browser-compat-db, a SQLite database conversion of Mozilla's browser compatibility data. The project includes Claude-generated Python scripts for data transformation, a GitHub Actions workflow built with GPT-4.5, and makes the database publicly accessible via GitHub CDN with CORS support for exploration via Datasette Lite.

item.title: What we learned from 1,604 Chinese AI job postings
item.description: Epoch AI: Epoch AI analyzed 1,604 Chinese AI job postings to infer the research strategies and priorities of Chinese AI labs. The analysis uses hiring patterns as a window into organizational focus areas and technology direction.

item.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Former Infosys CEO Vishal Sikka has launched a new startup backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures. The company brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI to challenge the traditional IT services sector.

item.title: Anthropic Just Replaced Claude Code With New Claude Tag
item.description: AI Revolution: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a team-based AI agent for Slack that replaces the previous Claude Code chatbot. Claude Tag connects to company tools and enables task management in public channels, integrating AI workflows directly into team communication platforms.

item.title: Omnigent: Open-source meta harness for agents
item.description: chatmasta: Omnigent is an open-source framework that provides a meta harness for agents. It appears designed to standardize or simplify the development and deployment of AI agent systems.

item.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Cerebras, an AI chipmaker, reported lower-than-expected gross margins in its first earnings as a public company, causing its stock price to decline. The CEO attributed the market reaction to a misunderstanding of the company's margin outlook.

item.title: A24 Knows You're Mad About the Google AI Collab
item.description: nate: A24 is facing backlash from its audience over a collaboration with Google AI. The announcement has generated negative public sentiment regarding the entertainment company's partnership with the tech giant.
